---
title: "Indexing A Batch"
---

---
title: "Indexing a Batch"
source: "https://support.hyland.com/r/Current/Hyland-Capture/fwz1646248501201"
section: "Hyland Capture"
---

If you captured documents in a Content Queue that has been configured to bypass the Hyland Capture Verify Data interface, you can index the batch using the Metadata Panel on the right side of the document review layout. This prepares the batch for direct export out of the Hyland Capture application.

When preparing to index a batch, also note the following:

-
If no fields have been assigned to any document classes in the batch's assigned Content Queue, the batch does not require indexing and the Metadata Panel is not displayed. For information on submitting the batch for export, see Submitting a Batch.

-
If any fields have been configured for bar code extraction and assigned to document classes in the batch's assigned Content Queue, metadata values are automatically extracted from bar codes on the captured documents and assigned to the corresponding fields during batch processing. Unclassified documents do not undergo bar code extraction processing.

To index a batch:

1.  Open the Hyland Capture application and log in (see Opening the Hyland Capture Application). The Hyland Capture Scan and Import interface is displayed, showing one of the following layouts:
-
If there are no pending batches, the document capture layout is displayed.

-
If there is a pending batch, the document review layout is displayed.

Note:
If you are already logged in to the Hyland Capture application and using the Verify Data interface, you must switch to the Scan and Import interface (see Switching the Interface).

2.  If there are no pending batches, capture documents to create a new batch (see Capturing Documents). The document review layout is displayed.
3.  In the thumbnail viewer to the right of the document viewer, set the focus to the document you want to index (see Organizing Captured Documents).
4.  To expand the Metadata Panel on the right side of the layout, click the Show Document Fields icon.   ![](data:image/gif;base64,)
To collapse the Metadata Panel along the right side of the layout, click the Hide Document Fields icon.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
5.  To classify or reclassify the document, do one of the following:
-
In the thumbnail viewer, select a document class from the classification drop-down list in the upper-left corner of the document bar. You can expand this drop-down list by clicking its arrow or pressing F7.

-
In the expanded Metadata Panel, select a document class from the Classification drop-down list near the top. You can expand this drop-down list by clicking its arrow.

When reclassifying a document, also note the following:

-
Each time you change the document's classification, previously entered metadata values are retained for common fields or cleared for unique fields. Fields that have the same name but different definitions or IDs are considered unique.

- Manually changing a document class does not trigger any additional bar code extraction processing, if applicable, for the document.
Once you select a document class for the document, the metadata fields associated with that document class are displayed in the Metadata Panel, as shown in the following example:

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
The document's local ID is displayed at the top of the panel.

6.  Enter valid metadata values for the document in the corresponding fields of the Metadata Panel. If an invalid value is entered, or if no value is entered for a required field, an error message is displayed in red text below the field. For more information on metadata handling and validation, see Metadata Handling and Validation.
7.  To save the entered value for a metadata field, defocus the field by doing one of the following:
-
Click outside the field.

-
Press Tab to shift the focus to the next field on the document.

-
Press Enter to shift the focus to the next field requiring review in the document, or to the next field requiring review in the batch if no remaining fields in the document require review. A field requires review if it contains an invalid value or if it is a required field that contains no value.

When working with saved metadata values, also note the following:

-
If you split pages from the current document into a new document, any assigned document class or metadata values from the original document are also applied to the new document.

-
If you merge pages from the current document into another document in the batch, the merged pages inherit any existing document class or metadata values from the destination document.

8.  Index additional documents in the batch as needed. Once all entered metadata is valid and all required metadata fields on all documents in the batch have been indexed, you can submit the batch for export (see Submitting a Batch).
